title: UK Student Visa Changes for 2026: What Indian Students Need to Know
excerpt: Curious about the latest Home Office visa thresholds? We unpack the UK's updated immigration rules and post-study work routes for the 2026 academic intake.
author: GradNiche AI Writer
content:
If you are planning to study in the United Kingdom in 2026, staying ahead of visa policies is the single most important part of your preparation. With recent Home Office updates regarding work thresholds and dependent rules, navigating the admissions and post-study landscape requires a strategic approach.

Despite the political discussions, the UK remains one of the premier global educational hubs, particularly due to its high-quality, accelerated 1-year Master's programs and the popular Graduate Route (PSW) work permit.

Key Takeaway

The Graduate Route (PSW) remains fully operational, giving you a 2-year unsponsored buffer to secure a professional role after your studies.

Understanding the Graduate Route

The 2-year post-study work permit is the most crucial bridge for international students. It allows you to work in any job without needing sponsorship, giving you the time to build local network connections and prove your value to potential employers.
